title: Part III: Insects and Arthropods
abstract: Insects and related arthropod pests occupy every conceivable niche on the peanut plant.  Insects feed in terminal buds and flowers; on leaves, roots, and pods; behind leaf axils and petioles; on plant fluids by inserting their mouthparts into cells of directly into the nutrient transport system; in tunnels that they form in the leaves, main stem, lateral branches or roots; and on or in peanut pods and seed...
